benchmark: CIS Amazon Web Services Foundations Benchmark
source_note: Reference total from AWS Security Hub CIS v5.0 standard (40 Level 1 controls). Vigil automates a curated subset via cis_aws_l1 mappings plus checks listed as extended_coverage.

controls.title: Maintain current contact details
Ensure security contact information is registered
Ensure no root user access key exists
Ensure MFA is enabled for the root user
Ensure hardware MFA is enabled for the root user
controls.note: Vigil verifies virtual or hardware MFA on root via account summary; hardware-only attestation is manual

controls.title: Eliminate use of the root user for administrative tasks
Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater
Ensure IAM password policy prevents password reuse
Ensure MFA is enabled for all IAM users with console password
Do not create access keys during initial user setup
Ensure credentials unused for 45 days or greater are disabled
Ensure there is only one active access key per IAM user
Ensure access keys are rotated every 90 days or less
Ensure IAM users receive permissions only through groups
Ensure IAM policies with full admin privileges are not attached
Ensure a support role exists to manage incidents with AWS Support
Ensure IAM instance roles are used for AWS resource access
Ensure expired SSL/TLS certificates in IAM are removed
Ensure IAM Access Analyzer is enabled in all regions
Ensure IAM password policy expires passwords within 90 days
Ensure access to AWSCloudShellFullAccess is restricted
Ensure no customer-managed policy allows full admin (*:*)
Ensure S3 bucket policies deny HTTP requests
Ensure S3 buckets have default encryption enabled
Ensure S3 bucket MFA Delete is enabled
Ensure S3 Block Public Access is enabled at account and bucket level
Ensure EBS volume encryption is enabled
Ensure RDS storage is encrypted
Ensure RDS instances are not publicly accessible
Ensure RDS automated backups are enabled
Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
Ensure CloudTrail log file validation is enabled
Ensure CloudTrail logs are encrypted at rest with KMS
Ensure S3 bucket access logging on CloudTrail bucket
Ensure AWS Config is enabled
Ensure S3 bucket used for CloudTrail is not publicly accessible
Ensure KMS key rotation is enabled
Ensure unauthorized SSH/RDP access is restricted
Ensure default security groups restrict all traffic
Ensure VPC flow logging is enabled
Ensure EC2 instances use IMDSv2
Ensure EBS default encryption is enabled
